entries.definition:
كتاب السّين

سكف - سلم

(سلم) السين واللام والميم معظم بابه من الصّحّة والعافية؛ ويكون فيه ما يشذُّ، والشاذُّ عنه قليل، فالسّلامة: أن يسلم الإنسان من العاهة والأذَى. قال أهلُ العلم: الله جلَّ ثناؤُه هو السلام؛ لسلامته مما يلحق المخلوقين من العيب والنقص والفناء. قال الله جلَّ جلاله: ] واللهُ يَدْعُو إلى دَارِ السَّلاَمِ [ [يونس 25]، فالسلام الله جلَّ ثناؤه، ودارُهُ الجنَّة. ومن الباب أيضاً الإسلام، وهو الانقياد؛ لأنَّه يَسْلم من الإباء والامتناع. والسِّلام: المسالمة. وفِعالٌ تجيءُ في المفاعلة كثيراً نحو القتال والمقاتلة. ومن باب الإصحاب والانقياد: السَّلَم الذي يسمَّى السَّلف، كأنَّه مالٌ أسلم ولم يمتنع من إعطائه. وممكن أن تكون الحجارة سمِّيت سِلاماً لأنّها أبعدُ شيء في الأرض من الفَناء والذَّهابِ؛ لشدّتها

سلو/ي

وصلابتها. فأمّا السَّليم وهو اللّديغ ففي تسميته قولان: أحدهما أنَّه أُسلم لما به. والقول الآخر أنّهم تفاءلوا بالسَّلامة. وقد يسمُّون الشيءَ بأسماء في التفاؤُل والتطيُّر. والسُّلَّم معروف، وهو من السلامة أيضاً؛ لأنَّ النازل عليه يُرْجَى لـه السَّلامة. والسَّلامة: شجر، وجمعها سَلاَم.

والذي شذَّ عن الباب السَّلْم: الدلو التي لها عروة واحدة. والسَّلَم: شجر، واحدته سَلَمة. والسَّلامانُ: شجرٌ .

ومن الباب الأول السَِّلْم وهو الصُّلح، وقد يؤنَّث ويذكَّر. قال الله تعالى: ] وإِنْ جَنَحُوا لِلْسَّلْمِ فَاجْنَحْ لَهَا [ [الأنفال 61]. والسَّلِمَة: الحجر، فيه يقول الشاعر:

يَرمِي ورائيَ بالسهمِ والسَّلِمَهْ

ذاكَ خليلي وذو يعاتِبُني

وبنو سلِمَة: بطنٌ من الأنصار ليس في العرب غيرهم. ومن الأسماء سَلْمَى: امرأةٌ. وسلمى: جبلٌ. وأبو سُلمى أبو زُهَير، بضم السين، ليس في العرب غيره.

(سلو/ي) السين واللام والحرف المعتلّ أصلٌ واحدٌ يدلُّ على خفض وطيب عيش. ومن ذلك قولهم فلان في سَلْوَةٍ من العيشِ، أي في رَغدٍ يسلِّيهِ الهم. ويقول: سَلاَ المحب يَسْلوا سُلُوَّاً، وذلك إذا فارقه ما كان به من همٍّ

s–l–m peace, safety, tranquillity; completeness; being free from obstacles; to submit to, to become resigned to; to hand over; ladder, staircase; to receive, to stroke, finger bones. Of this root, 18 forms occur 140 times in the Qurʾan: سلّم sallama six times; أسلم ʾaslama 22 times; سلم silm once; سلم salm twice; سلام salām five times; سالمون sālimūn once; سلام salām 42 times; سليم salīm twice; إسلام ʾislām eight times; مسلم muslim twice; مسلمين muslimayn once; مسلمون muslimūn 36 times; مسلمة muslimatun once; مسلمات muslimāt twice; مسلّمة musallamatun three times; تسليم taslīm three times; مستسلمون mustaslimūn once and سلّم sallam twice.
سلّم sallama I [v. II, intrans.] 1 to submit to a verdict, to give in (4:65) ثُمَّ لَا يَجِدُوا فِى أَنفُسِهِمْ حَرَجًا مِّمَّا قَضَيْتَ وَيُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا and then they will find no constraint in their hearts regarding your judgement, and yield completely 2 [with prep. على] to greet, to salute (24:27) وَسَلِّمُوا عَلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهَا and greet their inhabitants; *(33:56) صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا bless him [the Prophet] and salute him with greetings of peace (or, surrender yourselves completely to his guidance) 3 [with no notion of an obj.] to spare [from a bad outcome], to bail out, to save the day (8:43) وَلَوْ أَرَىٰكَهُمْ كَثِيرًا لَّفَشِلْتُمْ وَلَتَنَـٰزَعْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَمْرِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَلَّمَ had He shown them to you as many, you would certainly have lost heart and disputed about the matter, but God saved the day II [v. II, trans.] to hand over (2:233) وَإِنْ أَرَدتُّمْ أَن تَسْتَرْضِعُوٓا أَوْلَـٰدَكُمْ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذَا سَلَّمْتُم مَّآ ءَاتَيْتُم بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ and if you wish to have your children suckled [by someone other than the mother] there will be no blame attached to you, provided you pay an agreed amount in a fair manner.

أَسْلَمَ ʾaslama I [v. IV, intrans.] 1 to surrender, to submit, to devote oneself (3:83) أَفَغَيْرَ دِينِ ٱللَّهِ يَبْغُونَ وَلَهُۥٓ أَسْلَمَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ

451 س/ل/م

Everyone in the heavens and earth submits to Him willingly or unwillingly 2 to resign oneself, to come to accept (37:103) فَلَمَّا أَسْلَمَا وَتَلَّهُ لِلْجَبِينِ so when they came to accept and he laid him upon his forehead 3 to profess the religion of Islam, to become a Muslim (49:17) يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ أَسْلَمُوا they count it as a favour to you [Prophet], that they have become Muslims II [v. IV, trans.] *(2:112) مَنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ he who submits his whole being to God [lit. face].

سِلْم silm [n.] peace; self-surrender (a large number of commentators, however, interpret this word as meaning ‘the religion of Islam’ in spite of contextual incompatibility) (2:208) يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا ٱدْخُلُوا فِى ٱلسِّلْمِ كَآفَّةً you who believe, enter wholeheartedly into complete submission to God.

سَلْم salm [n.] peace (8:61) وَإِن جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَٱجْنَحْ لَهَا but if they lean towards peace, then lean towards it [as well].

سَلَم salām I [n.] peace, end of hostility (4:90) فَإِنِ ٱعْتَزَلُوكُمْ فَلَمْ يُقَـٰتِلُوكُمْ وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلسَّلَمَ so if they leave you alone and do not fight you, and offer you peace, then God gives you no way against them II [quasi-pass. part.] wholly devoted to, wholly belonging to (39:29) ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا رَّجُلًا فِيهِ شُرَكَآءُ مُتَشَـٰكِسُونَ وَرَجُلًا سَلَمًا لِّرَجُلٍ God sets forth a parable–of a man belonging to partners who are at odds with one another, and a man belonging wholly to one man.

سالمون sālimūn [pl. of act. part. سالم sālim] safe and sound, not threatened (68:43) وَقَدْ كَانُوا يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سَـٰلِمُونَ they were invited to prostrate themselves when they were safe [but refused].

سلام salām I [n.] 1 peace (5:16) يَهْدِى بِهِ ٱللَّهُ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ رِضْوَٰنَهُۥ سُبُلَ ٱلسَّلَـٰمِ With which God guides those who follow what pleases Him to the paths of peace; *(59:23) ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْقُدُّوسُ ٱلسَّلَـٰمُ the King, the Holy One, the Source of Peace; *(6:127) دَارُ ٱلسَّلَـٰمِ Paradise [lit. the house of peace] 2 safety, security (21:69) قُلْنَا يَـٰنَارُ كُونِى بَرْدًا وَسَلَـٰمًا عَلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ [but] We said, ‘Fire, be coolness and safety for Abraham’ 3 greeting of peace (56:91) فَسَلَـٰمٌ لَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْيَمِينِ and so ‘Peace be on you’ [will be said to you] by the companions on the Right; *(6:54) سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ‘Peace be upon you’ II [adverbially] 1 greeting

452 س/ل/م

of peace to you (51:25) إِذْ دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا سَلَـٰمًا they went in to see him and said, ‘greetings of peace’; *(56:26) إِلَّا قِيلًا سَلَـٰمًا سَلَـٰمًا only agreeable speech [will they hear in Paradise] [lit. but a saying of ‘Peace, peace’] 2 being clear, or quit, of (25:63) وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ ٱلْجَـٰهِلُونَ قَالُوا سَلَـٰمًا and when the ignorant speak to them, they say: ‘We have nothing to do with you [lit. are quit of you]’ (or, they say ‘in Peace’).

سليم salīm [quasi-act. part.] 1 pure, wholesome, sound, free of evil (26:89) إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى ٱللَّهَ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ except for the one who comes before God with a pure heart 2 sick, heavy, troubled (in one interpretation of 37:84) إِذْ جَآءَ رَبَّهُ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ when he came to his Lord with a troubled (or, a pure) heart.

إسلام ʾislām I [n.] total surrender (3:19) إِنَّ ٱلدِّينَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْإِسْلَـٰمُ True Religion, in God’s eyes, is total surrender [to Him] 2 [with the definite article الإسلام ʾal-ʾislām] the religion of Islam (5:3) ٱلْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِى وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ ٱلْإِسْلَـٰمَ دِينًا today I have perfected your religion for you, completed My blessing upon you, and sanctioned for you Islam [the total submission to God] as religion II [v. n.] (act of) surrendering, submitting (9:74) وَلَقَدْ قَالُوا كَلِمَةَ ٱلْكُفْرِ وَكَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِسْلَـٰمِهِمْ but they certainly did speak the word of disbelief and became disbelievers after having submitted.

مسلم muslim [act. part.] 1 one who submits [to God] (2:133) نَعْبُدُ إِلَـٰهَكَ وَإِلَـٰهَ ءَابَآئِكَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ إِلَـٰهًا وَٰحِدًا وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ we will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham, Ishmael and Isaac, one single God–we submit ourselves to Him 2 one who professes the faith of Islam (22:78) هُوَ سَمَّىٰكُمُ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ مِن قَبْلُ وَفِى هَـٰذَا He has called you Muslims–both in the past and in this [Book] 3 one showing obedience (27:38) أَيُّكُمْ يَأْتِينِى بِعَرْشِهَا قَبْلَ أَن يَأْتُونِى مُسْلِمِينَ which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in obedience [to my bidding]?

مسلّمة musallamatun [pass. part.] 1 free of faults, free of blemishes (2:71) إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا ذَلُولٌ تُثِيرُ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَلَا تَسْقِى ٱلْحَرْثَ مُسَلَّمَةٌ لَّا شِيَةَ فِيهَا it is a cow, not broken in to plough the earth or water the tillage, perfect and unblemished 2 handed over (4:92) وَدِيَةٌ مُّسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِ and compensation/blood money handed to his [the victim’s]

453 سليمان

folk.

تسليم taslīm [v. n. of v. II, adverbially used] 1 surrendering, yielding (4:65) ثُمَّ لَا يَجِدُوا فِى أَنفُسِهِمْ حَرَجًا مِّمَّا قَضَيْتَ وَيُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا and then they will find no constraint in their hearts regarding your judgement, and yield completely 2 greeting with the words ‘peace’ (33:56) صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا bless him [the Prophet] and salute him with greetings of peace (or, surrender yourselves completely to his guidance).

مستسلمون mustaslimūn [pl. of مُسْتَسْلِم mustaslim; act. part.] those submitting, surrendering (37:26) بَلْ هُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ مُسْتَسْلِمُونَ no indeed!, they will be in complete submission on this Day.

سُلَّم sullam [n.] ladder (52:38) أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ or do they have a ladder from which they [are able to] eavesdrop?

سليمان Sulaymān (see alphabetically).

أَسْلَمَ ʾaslama I [v. IV, intrans.] 1 to surrender, to submit, to devote oneself (3:83) أَفَغَيْرَ دِينِ ٱللَّهِ يَبْغُونَ وَلَهُۥٓ أَسْلَمَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ

451 س/ل/م

Everyone in the heavens and earth submits to Him willingly or unwillingly 2 to resign oneself, to come to accept (37:103) فَلَمَّا أَسْلَمَا وَتَلَّهُ لِلْجَبِينِ so when they came to accept and he laid him upon his forehead 3 to profess the religion of Islam, to become a Muslim (49:17) يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ أَسْلَمُوا they count it as a favour to you [Prophet], that they have become Muslims II [v. IV, trans.] *(2:112) مَنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ he who submits his whole being to God [lit. face].

سِلْم silm [n.] peace; self-surrender (a large number of commentators, however, interpret this word as meaning ‘the religion of Islam’ in spite of contextual incompatibility) (2:208) يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا ٱدْخُلُوا فِى ٱلسِّلْمِ كَآفَّةً you who believe, enter wholeheartedly into complete submission to God.

سَلْم salm [n.] peace (8:61) وَإِن جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَٱجْنَحْ لَهَا but if they lean towards peace, then lean towards it [as well].

سَلَم salām I [n.] peace, end of hostility (4:90) فَإِنِ ٱعْتَزَلُوكُمْ فَلَمْ يُقَـٰتِلُوكُمْ وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلسَّلَمَ so if they leave you alone and do not fight you, and offer you peace, then God gives you no way against them II [quasi-pass. part.] wholly devoted to, wholly belonging to (39:29) ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا رَّجُلًا فِيهِ شُرَكَآءُ مُتَشَـٰكِسُونَ وَرَجُلًا سَلَمًا لِّرَجُلٍ God sets forth a parable–of a man belonging to partners who are at odds with one another, and a man belonging wholly to one man.

سالمون sālimūn [pl. of act. part. سالم sālim] safe and sound, not threatened (68:43) وَقَدْ كَانُوا يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سَـٰلِمُونَ they were invited to prostrate themselves when they were safe [but refused].

سلام salām I [n.] 1 peace (5:16) يَهْدِى بِهِ ٱللَّهُ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ رِضْوَٰنَهُۥ سُبُلَ ٱلسَّلَـٰمِ With which God guides those who follow what pleases Him to the paths of peace; *(59:23) ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْقُدُّوسُ ٱلسَّلَـٰمُ the King, the Holy One, the Source of Peace; *(6:127) دَارُ ٱلسَّلَـٰمِ Paradise [lit. the house of peace] 2 safety, security (21:69) قُلْنَا يَـٰنَارُ كُونِى بَرْدًا وَسَلَـٰمًا عَلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ [but] We said, ‘Fire, be coolness and safety for Abraham’ 3 greeting of peace (56:91) فَسَلَـٰمٌ لَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْيَمِينِ and so ‘Peace be on you’ [will be said to you] by the companions on the Right; *(6:54) سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ‘Peace be upon you’ II [adverbially] 1 greeting

452 س/ل/م

of peace to you (51:25) إِذْ دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا سَلَـٰمًا they went in to see him and said, ‘greetings of peace’; *(56:26) إِلَّا قِيلًا سَلَـٰمًا سَلَـٰمًا only agreeable speech [will they hear in Paradise] [lit. but a saying of ‘Peace, peace’] 2 being clear, or quit, of (25:63) وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ ٱلْجَـٰهِلُونَ قَالُوا سَلَـٰمًا and when the ignorant speak to them, they say: ‘We have nothing to do with you [lit. are quit of you]’ (or, they say ‘in Peace’).

سليم salīm [quasi-act. part.] 1 pure, wholesome, sound, free of evil (26:89) إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى ٱللَّهَ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ except for the one who comes before God with a pure heart 2 sick, heavy, troubled (in one interpretation of 37:84) إِذْ جَآءَ رَبَّهُ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ when he came to his Lord with a troubled (or, a pure) heart.

إسلام ʾislām I [n.] total surrender (3:19) إِنَّ ٱلدِّينَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْإِسْلَـٰمُ True Religion, in God’s eyes, is total surrender [to Him] 2 [with the definite article الإسلام ʾal-ʾislām] the religion of Islam (5:3) ٱلْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِى وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ ٱلْإِسْلَـٰمَ دِينًا today I have perfected your religion for you, completed My blessing upon you, and sanctioned for you Islam [the total submission to God] as religion II [v. n.] (act of) surrendering, submitting (9:74) وَلَقَدْ قَالُوا كَلِمَةَ ٱلْكُفْرِ وَكَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِسْلَـٰمِهِمْ but they certainly did speak the word of disbelief and became disbelievers after having submitted.

مسلم muslim [act. part.] 1 one who submits [to God] (2:133) نَعْبُدُ إِلَـٰهَكَ وَإِلَـٰهَ ءَابَآئِكَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ إِلَـٰهًا وَٰحِدًا وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ we will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham, Ishmael and Isaac, one single God–we submit ourselves to Him 2 one who professes the faith of Islam (22:78) هُوَ سَمَّىٰكُمُ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ مِن قَبْلُ وَفِى هَـٰذَا He has called you Muslims–both in the past and in this [Book] 3 one showing obedience (27:38) أَيُّكُمْ يَأْتِينِى بِعَرْشِهَا قَبْلَ أَن يَأْتُونِى مُسْلِمِينَ which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in obedience [to my bidding]?

مسلّمة musallamatun [pass. part.] 1 free of faults, free of blemishes (2:71) إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا ذَلُولٌ تُثِيرُ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَلَا تَسْقِى ٱلْحَرْثَ مُسَلَّمَةٌ لَّا شِيَةَ فِيهَا it is a cow, not broken in to plough the earth or water the tillage, perfect and unblemished 2 handed over (4:92) وَدِيَةٌ مُّسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِ and compensation/blood money handed to his [the victim’s]

453 سليمان

folk.

تسليم taslīm [v. n. of v. II, adverbially used] 1 surrendering, yielding (4:65) ثُمَّ لَا يَجِدُوا فِى أَنفُسِهِمْ حَرَجًا مِّمَّا قَضَيْتَ وَيُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا and then they will find no constraint in their hearts regarding your judgement, and yield completely 2 greeting with the words ‘peace’ (33:56) صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا bless him [the Prophet] and salute him with greetings of peace (or, surrender yourselves completely to his guidance).

مستسلمون mustaslimūn [pl. of مُسْتَسْلِم mustaslim; act. part.] those submitting, surrendering (37:26) بَلْ هُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ مُسْتَسْلِمُونَ no indeed!, they will be in complete submission on this Day.

سُلَّم sullam [n.] ladder (52:38) أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ or do they have a ladder from which they [are able to] eavesdrop?

سليمان Sulaymān (see alphabetically).
